title: Chronic Pancreatitis with Pseudocyst Complicated by Splenic Abscess
description: A 53-year-old African American male with chronic pancreatitis, insulin-dependent diabetes, hypertension, peripheral arterial disease, and gout presented with acute abdominal pain from an enlarging pancreatic pseudocyst. After endoscopic cystogastrostomy, he developed respiratory distress, pleural effusion, and a splenic abscess requiring splenectomy. The case tests medication normalization across a nine-drug polypharmacy regimen and multi-system differential diagnosis reasoning.
notes: Derived from dataset row idx 54828. The original dataset row is copied into case_10_source.json. All patient data comes from the synthetic augmented-clinical-notes dataset. The primary challenge is the nine-medication polypharmacy regimen and the evolving clinical picture from pseudocyst to splenic complications.
